html:
Exclusive to:  

Watermelon is a Fruit Food item from the Don't Starve: Reign of Giants DLC. It can be harvested from a Farm with an 11% percent chance of being grown from Seeds. It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes, particularly Melonsicles. Feeding a Watermelon to Birds in a Birdcage will produce 1–2 Watermelon Seeds. When eaten, it will also decrease the perceived temperature by 40 degrees for 5 seconds. 

It may also be combined with Twigs at an Alchemy Engine to make a Fashion Melon, which will provide unpleasant protection against Overheating in Summer.

Downloadable Content

Watermelons are also included in the Don't Starve: Shipwrecked and Hamlet DLCs, where they can be acquired through farming. In Hamlet, Watermelons can be bought for an Oinc at the Swinesbury Fine Grocer's.

Grilled Watermelon

Grilled Watermelon is a Fruit Food item made by Cooking a Watermelon on a Campfire, Fire Pit, or Star. It restores slightly more Sanity than its raw version but heals slightly less. 

Although it no longer fits the requirements for Melonsicles and Fashion Melons, it can still fit other Fruit based Crock Pot recipes.

Trivia

  • Watermelons are the only farm food added in Reign of Giants.
  • As of the She Sells Sea Shells update for Don't Starve Together, Willow's examination quote for watermelon and grilled watermelon were changed to avoid confusion with the new character. Her quote for watermelon was changed from "I once knew a melon named Walter. He was a jerk." to "But where's the firemelon?" , and her quote for grilled watermelon was changed from "Warm, sweet, juicy Walter." to "I made my own firemelon." These quotes were changed to avoid confusion over the new character, Walter, though the old quotes are still present in Singleplayer. Joe W further confirmed that Willow did not know Walter on the Klei forums.

Gallery

  • Fashion Melons in one of the Rhymes with Play streams on Twitch.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord

"watermelon"
“Sticky sweet.”
–Wilson, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“I once knew a melon named Walter. He was a jerk.”
–Willow, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“So many seeds.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“It's mostly water. What's the big deal?”
–Wendy, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“SPHERE OF JUICE”
–WX-78, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Citrullus lanatus.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“How can it be both water and melon?”
–Woodie, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“It's mostly water. Fibrous, sweet water.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Too sweet for my stomach.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“It makes a gööd söund when yöu hit it.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Looks tasty!”
–Webber, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Nothing better on a hot day!”
–Walani, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Despite its name, it is mostly filled with deliciousness!”
–Warly, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Aye th'fruit after Woodleg's watery heart.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“'TIS BALL O' JUICE”
–Wilba, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Watery sweet belly stuff”
–Wormwood, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“A big ball of fruit.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Watermelon in Don't Starve.
Watermelon is a Fruit Food item from the Don't Starve: Reign of Giants DLC. It can be harvested from a Farm with an 11% percent chance of being grown from Seeds. It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes, particularly Melonsicles. Feeding a Watermelon to Birds in a Birdcage will produce 1–2 Watermelon Seeds. When eaten, it will also decrease the perceived temperature by 40 degrees for 5 seconds.
It may also be combined with Twigs at an Alchemy Engine to make a Fashion Melon, which will provide unpleasant protection against Overheating in Summer.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
Watermelons are also included in the Don't Starve: Shipwrecked and Hamlet DLCs, where they can be acquired through farming.
In Hamlet, Watermelons can be bought for an Oinc at the Swinesbury Fine Grocer's.

“Juicy and warm.”
–Wilson, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Warm, sweet, juicy Walter.”
–Willow, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“I cook seeds out of melon.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Now it's warm water.”
–Wendy, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“HOT JUICE”
–WX-78, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Beautifully grilled.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“I'm not sure aboot grilled fruit...”
–Woodie, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Grillermelon.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“I am able to consume it in this form.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Warm and red, but it döesn't flöw.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“We can cook anything!”
–Webber, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Hot watermelon...”
–Walani, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Could use mint and feta...”
–Warly, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Better'n me thought it'd be.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“WARM'D NUM NUM FRUITS”
–Wilba, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Slurpy”
–Wormwood, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.
“Are those grill marks or big seeds?”
–Wheeler, when examining a Grilled Watermelon in Don't Starve.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Dưa Hấu là một Thực Phẩm Trái Cây từ DLC Don't Starve: Reign of Giants. Nó có thể được thu hoạch từ Trang trại với 11% cơ hội được trồng từ Hạt giống. Nó có thể được dùng làm nguyên liệu trong một số công thức nấu Nồi Hầm, đặc biệt là Dưa. Cho Chim trong Lồng Chim ăn Dưa Hấu sẽ tạo ra 1–2 Hạt Dưa Hấu. Khi ăn, nó cũng sẽ giảm nhiệt độ cảm nhận xuống 40 độ trong 5 giây.
